posts.name: Career change: My journey into tech
content: After studying at university for four and a half years, I had no plans to return to school. I was done with that chapter of my life. Since I had never quite found my niche regarding my working life, I chose a broad degree — a Bachelor of Science in Industrial Engineering and Management — hoping that somewhere along the way I would discover in what direction I wanted to go.
My father has worked as an IT consultant for many years, so I grew up watching him type green numbers on a black screen — something I always thought looked incredibly boring.
After graduating, my journey toward finding the right professional role began. I tried different jobs, but none of them felt quite right.
A few years after earning my degree, I started working as an IT strategist. The work felt meaningful, but I soon realized I wanted to understand more about the technical side of IT.
That’s when the JavaScript Development Bootcamp by Technigo appeared. It was a 32-week bootcamp, and it felt perfect.
A few weeks later, the results came in — I had been accepted.
So here we are, at the end of the bootcamp, and I haven’t regretted it for a single day.
